prompt: A surreal, impossible architectural scene representing a logical paradox: two separate staircases on either side of the image, each clearly ascending upward with small human figures walking confidently upward. In the center, the two staircases merge into a single larger structure that visibly slopes downward, with the same figures now descending. The perspective is physically impossible but visually convincing, inspired by M.C. Escher-style spatial paradoxes. Smooth stone textures, soft shadows, no text, no symbols, no numbers. Emphasize contradiction: local upward motion vs global downward direction. Clean composition, minimal background, focus on structure and movement, uncanny and unsettling atmosphere.
visual_logic: Two separate upward paths merge into a single downward structure, showing how local trends reverse when combined into a global perspective.
